title: The Effect of Mindfulness-based Programs on Cognitive Function in Adults: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
headline: The Effect of Mindfulness-based Programs on Cognitive Function in Adults: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysi
summary: A meta-analysis / systematic review of mindfulness / meditation examined focus & attention. Mindfulness-based programs (MBPs) are increasingly utilized to improve mental health.
keyFindings: Mindfulness-based programs (MBPs) are increasingly utilized to improve mental health.
Interest in the putative effects of MBPs on cognitive function is also growing.
limitations: Several of the pooled studies had methodological weaknesses, which lowers confidence in the result.
The evidence is limited or mixed, so firm conclusions are not yet warranted.
Pooled estimates combine heterogeneous studies; effect sizes vary by practice, population, and control condition.

The practice studied (mindfulness / meditation) is not the Miracle of Mind practice, so its findings don't transfer directly — though the shared directed-attention component may make some transfer plausible (see “Why this matters for the 7-minute practice” below).
simplifiedTitle: The Effect of Mindfulness-based Programs on Cognitive Function in Adults: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysi
aiSnippet: Meta-analysis / Systematic review, Neuropsychology review (2022). Mindfulness-based programs (MBPs) are increasingly utilized to improve mental health. Interest in the putative effects of MBPs on cognitive function is also growing.
whyItMatters: MoM shares directed attention (the focused-attention component) with the practice studied here. Because the benefit is linked to that mechanism rather than to any one method, it's reasonable to expect it may extend to MoM as well — though MoM hasn't been studied directly for this outcome.
